california_schools||27.question: What is the average score in writing for the schools that were opened after 1991 or closed before 2000? List the school names along with the score. Also, list the communication number of the schools if there is any.

financial||116.question: For the client whose loan was approved first in 1993/7/5, what is the increase rate of his/her account balance from 1993/3/22 to 1998/12/27?
financial||116.evidence: Increase rate of his/her account balance = [(balance of date A - balance of date B) / balance of Date B] * 100%
financial||116.gold: SELECT CAST((SUM(IIF(T3.date = '1998-12-27', T3.balance, 0)) - SUM(IIF(T3.date = '1993-03-22', T3.balance, 0))) AS REAL) * 100 / SUM(IIF(T3.date = '1993-03-22', T3.balance, 0)) FROM loan AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id INNER JOIN trans AS T3 ON T3.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE T1.date = '1993-07-05'

financial||117.question: What is the percentage of loan amount that has been fully paid with no issue.
financial||117.evidence: Loan paid with no issue means contract finished, no problems; status = 'A' means contract finished, no problems; Percentage of accounts by condition = [(total(amount) & condition) / (total amount)] * 100%
financial||117.gold: SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'A' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100) / SUM(amount) FROM loan
samples: SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'A' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) * 100.0) / SUM(amount) AS percentage_fully_paid FROM loan;
SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'A' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) / SUM(amount) AS percentage_fully_paid FROM loan;
SELECT ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'A' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / SUM(amount), 2) AS percentage_fully_paid FROM loan;
SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'A' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) * 100.0) / SUM(amount) AS percentage_fully_paid FROM loan;
SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'A' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) * 100.0) / SUM(amount) AS percentage_fully_paid_no_issue FROM loan;
SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(amount) FILTER (WHERE status = 'A') / SUM(amount), 2) AS percentage_fully_paid FROM loan;
SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'A' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) * 100.0) / SUM(amount) AS percentage_fully_paid FROM loan;
SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'A' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) * 100.0) / SUM(amount) AS percentage_fully_paid FROM loan;

financial||118.question: For loan amount less than USD100,000, what is the percentage of accounts that is still running with no issue.
financial||118.evidence: Status = 'C' stands for running contract, ok so far; Percentage of accounts by condition = [(total(amount) & condition) / (total amount)] * 100.
financial||118.gold: SELECT CAST(SUM(status = 'C') AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(account_id) FROM loan WHERE amount < 100000
samples: SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN status = 'C' THEN account_id END) * 100.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT account_id)) AS running_percentage FROM loan WHERE amount < 100000;
SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'C' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS percentage_running FROM loan WHERE amount < 100000;
SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN l.status = 'C' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) / COUNT(*)) * 100 AS percentage_running FROM loan l WHERE l.amount < 100000;
SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN l.status = 'C' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS percentage_running FROM loan l WHERE l.amount < 100000;
SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN loan.status = 'C' AND loan.amount < 100000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0) / COUNT(*) AS percentage_running_loans_under_100k FROM loan WHERE loan.amount < 100000;
SELECT 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN l.status = 'C' THEN l.amount ELSE 0 END) / SUM(l.amount) AS percentage_running FROM loan l WHERE l.amount < 100000;
SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN l.status = 'C' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) / COUNT(*)) * 100 AS percentage_running FROM loan l WHERE l.amount < 100000;

SELECT (COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN status = 'C' AND amount < 100000 THEN account_id END) * 100.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN amount < 100000 THEN account_id END)) AS running_percentage FROM loan WHERE amount < 100000;

financial||125.question: For loans contracts which are still running where client are in debt, list the district of the and the state the percentage unemployment rate increment from year 1995 to 1996.
financial||125.evidence: Unemployment increment rate in percentage = [(unemployment rate 2016 - unemployment rate 2015) / unemployment rate 2015] * 100; unemployment rate 2015 appears in the A12; unemployment rate 2016 appears in the A13; Loan contracts which are still running where client are in debt can be presented as status = 'D'
financial||125.gold: SELECT CAST((T3.A13 - T3.A12) AS REAL) * 100 / T3.A12 FROM loan AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id INNER JOIN district AS T3 ON T2.district_id = T3.district_id WHERE T1.status = 'D'

financial||129.question: Which are the top ten withdrawals (non-credit card) by district names for the month of January 1996?
financial||129.evidence: Non-credit card withdraws refers to type = 'VYDAJ'; January 1996 can be found by date LIKE '1996-01%' in the database; A2 means district names
financial||129.gold: SELECT DISTINCT T1.A2 FROM district AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id INNER JOIN trans AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T3.type = 'VYDAJ' AND T3.date LIKE '1996-01%' ORDER BY A2 ASC LIMIT 10
samples: SELECT d.A2, SUM(t.amount) AS total_withdrawals FROM trans t JOIN account a ON t.account_id = a.account_id JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id WHERE t.type = 'VYDAJ' AND t.date LIKE '1996-01%' GROUP BY d.A2 ORDER BY total_withdrawals DESC LIMIT 10;

SELECT d.A2, SUM(t.amount) AS total_withdrawal FROM trans t JOIN account a ON t.account_id = a.account_id JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id JOIN card c ON c.account_id = t.account_id WHERE t.type = 'VYDAJ' AND c.type != 'CREDIT' AND t.date LIKE '1996-01%' GROUP BY d.A2 ORDER BY total_withdrawal DESC LIMIT 10;

SELECT d.A2, SUM(t.amount) AS total_withdrawals FROM trans t JOIN account a ON t.account_id = a.account_id JOIN disp disp ON a.account_id = disp.account_id JOIN card c ON disp.disp_id = c.disp_id LEFT JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id WHERE t.type = 'VYDAJ' AND (c.type IS NULL OR c.type != 'CREDIT') AND t.date LIKE '1996-01%' GROUP BY d.A2 ORDER BY total_withdrawals DESC LIMIT 10;

SELECT d.A2 AS district_name, SUM(t.amount) AS total_withdrawals FROM trans t JOIN account a ON t.account_id = a.account_id JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id WHERE t.type = 'VYDAJ' AND t.date LIKE '1996-01%' GROUP BY d.A2 ORDER BY total_withdrawals DESC LIMIT 10;

SELECT d.A2 AS district_name, SUM(t.amount) AS total_withdrawals FROM trans t JOIN account a ON t.account_id = a.account_id JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id JOIN card c ON a.account_id = c.disp_id WHERE t.type = 'VYDAJ' AND t.date LIKE '1996-01%' AND c.type != 'credit' GROUP BY d.A2 ORDER BY total_withdrawals DESC LIMIT 10;

SELECT d.A2, SUM(t.amount) AS total_withdrawals FROM trans t JOIN account a ON t.account_id = a.account_id JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id JOIN card c ON a.account_id = c.disp_id WHERE t.type = 'VYDAJ' AND t.date LIKE '1996-01%' AND c.type != 'CREDIT' GROUP BY d.A2 ORDER BY total_withdrawals DESC LIMIT 10;

SELECT d.A2, SUM(t.amount) AS total_withdrawals FROM trans t JOIN account a ON t.account_id = a.account_id JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id WHERE t.type = 'VYDAJ' AND t.date LIKE '1996-01%' GROUP BY d.A2 ORDER BY total_withdrawals DESC LIMIT 10;

SELECT d.A2, SUM(t.amount) AS total_withdrawals FROM trans t JOIN account a ON t.account_id = a.account_id JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id WHERE t.type = 'VYDAJ' AND t.date LIKE '1996-01%' GROUP BY d.A2 ORDER BY total_withdrawals DESC LIMIT 10;

financial||131.question: Which district has highest active loan?
financial||131.evidence: A3 refers to district names; Active loan refers to running contracts; Status = 'C' stands for running contract, OK so far; Status = 'D' stands for running contract, client in debt
financial||131.gold: SELECT T2.A3 FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id INNER JOIN loan AS T3 ON T1.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T3.status IN ('C', 'D') GROUP BY T2.A3 ORDER BY SUM(T3.amount) DESC LIMIT 1
samples: SELECT d.A3 FROM district d JOIN account a ON d.district_id = a.district_id JOIN loan l ON a.account_id = l.account_id WHERE l.status IN ('C', 'D') GROUP BY d.A3 ORDER BY SUM(l.amount) DESC LIMIT 1;

SELECT d.A3 FROM district d JOIN account a ON d.district_id = a.district_id JOIN loan l ON a.account_id = l.account_id WHERE l.status IN ('C', 'D') GROUP BY d.A3 ORDER BY SUM(l.amount) DESC LIMIT 1;

SELECT district.A3 FROM district JOIN account ON district.district_id = account.district_id JOIN loan ON account.account_id = loan.account_id WHERE loan.status IN ('C', 'D') GROUP BY district.district_id ORDER BY SUM(loan.amount) DESC LIMIT 1;
SELECT d.A3 FROM loan l JOIN account a ON l.account_id = a.account_id JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id WHERE l.status IN ('C', 'D') GROUP BY d.A3 ORDER BY SUM(l.amount) DESC LIMIT 1;

SELECT d.A3 AS district_name, SUM(l.amount) AS total_active_loan FROM loan l JOIN account a ON l.account_id = a.account_id JOIN district d ON a.district_id = d.district_id WHERE l.status IN ('C', 'D') GROUP BY d.district_id ORDER BY total_active_loan DESC LIMIT 1;

financial||169.question: What was the growth rate of the total amount of loans across all accounts for a male client between 1996 and 1997?

financial||185.question: What proportion of customers who have accounts at the Prague branch are female?

financial||186.question: What percentage of male clients request for weekly statements to be issued?
financial||186.evidence: Percentage of male clients = [count(male clients who requested weekly statements / count(clients who requested weekly statements)] * 100%; Male means gender = 'M'; 'POPLATEK TYDNE' stands for weekly issuance
financial||186.gold: SELECT CAST(SUM(T1.gender = 'M') AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.client_id) FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T3 ON T1.district_id = T3.district_id INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T2.district_id = T3.district_id INNER JOIN disp as T4 on T1.client_id = T4.client_id AND T2.account_id = T4.account_id WHERE T2.frequency = 'POPLATEK TYDNE'
samples: SELECT 100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT c.client_id) / (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT c2.client_id) FROM client c2 JOIN disp d2 ON c2.client_id = d2.client_id JOIN account a2 ON d2.account_id = a2.account_id WHERE a2.frequency = 'POPLATEK TYDNE') AS male_weekly_statement_percentage FROM client c JOIN disp d ON c.client_id = d.client_id JOIN account a ON d.account_id = a.account_id WHERE c.gender = 'M' AND a.frequency = 'POPLATEK TYDNE'

SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN c.gender = 'M' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) AS male_weekly_percentage FROM client c JOIN disp d ON c.client_id = d.client_id JOIN account a ON d.account_id = a.account_id WHERE a.frequency = 'POPLATEK TYDNE'

toxicology||218.question: What percentage of carcinogenic-type molecules does not contain fluorine?
toxicology||218.evidence: label = '+' mean molecules are carcinogenic; contain fluorine refers to element = 'f'; percentage = DIVIDE(SUM(element = 'f') * 100, COUNT(molecule_id)) where label = '+'; Should consider the distinct atoms when counting;
toxicology||218.gold: SELECT CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN T1.element <> 'f' THEN T2.molecule_id ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(DISTINCT T2.molecule_id) FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T2.label = '+'

SELECT 100.0 - (COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN atom.element = 'f' THEN atom.molecule_id END) * 100.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN molecule.label = '+' THEN molecule.molecule_id END)) FROM molecule LEFT JOIN atom ON molecule.molecule_id = atom.molecule_id WHERE molecule.label = '+';

formula_1||954.question: Please calculate the race completion percentage of Japanese drivers from 2007 to 2009.
formula_1||954.evidence: from 2007 to 2009 refers to year between 2007 and 2009; race completion refers to time is not null; percentage = Divide(COUNT(DriverID where time is not null and year between 2007 and 2009),Count (DriverID where year between 2007 and 2009))*100;
formula_1||954.gold: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.time IS NOT NULL, 1, 0)) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.raceId) FROM results AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 on T1.raceId = T2.raceId INNER JOIN drivers AS T3 on T1.driverId = T3.driverId WHERE T3.nationality = 'Japanese' AND T2.year BETWEEN 2007 AND 2009
samples: SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN r.time IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) / COUNT(r.driverId)) * 100 AS completion_percentage FROM results r JOIN races ra ON r.raceId = ra.raceId JOIN drivers d ON r.driverId = d.driverId WHERE d.nationality = 'Japanese' AND ra.year BETWEEN 2007 AND 2009;

SELECT 100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT r.driverId) FILTER (WHERE r.time IS NOT NULL) / COUNT(DISTINCT r.driverId) FROM results r JOIN races ra ON r.raceId = ra.raceId JOIN drivers d ON r.driverId = d.driverId WHERE d.nationality = 'Japanese' AND ra.year BETWEEN 2007 AND 2009;

SELECT (CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT r.driverId) AS FLOAT) * 100.0) / (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT driverId) FROM results res JOIN races ra ON res.raceId = ra.raceId WHERE ra.year BETWEEN 2007 AND 2009 AND res.driverId IN (SELECT driverId FROM drivers WHERE nationality = 'Japanese')) AS completion_percentage FROM results r JOIN races ra ON r.raceId = ra.raceId WHERE ra.year BETWEEN 2007 AND 2009 AND r.driverId IN (SELECT driverId FROM drivers WHERE nationality = 'Japanese') AND r.time IS NOT NULL;

formula_1||955.question: What is the average time in seconds of champion for each year, before year 1975?
formula_1||955.evidence: only champion's finished time is represented by 'HH:MM:SS.mmm'; finished the game refers to time is not null; before year 1975 refers to year < 1975;
formula_1||955.gold: WITH time_in_seconds AS ( SELECT T2.year, T2.raceId, T1.positionOrder, CASE WHEN T1.positionOrder = 1 THEN (CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 1, 1) AS REAL) * 3600) + (CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 3, 2) AS REAL) * 60) + CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 6,2) AS REAL )   + CAST(SUBSTR(T1.time, 9) AS REAL)/1000 ELSE 0 END AS time_seconds FROM results AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T1.raceId = T2.raceId WHERE T1.time IS NOT NULL ), champion_time AS ( SELECT year, raceId, time_seconds FROM time_in_seconds WHERE positionOrder = 1 ) SELECT year, AVG(time_seconds) FROM champion_time WHERE year < 1975 GROUP BY year HAVING AVG(time_seconds) IS NOT NULL
samples: SELECT races.year, AVG((CAST(SUBSTR(results.time,1,2) AS INTEGER)*3600 + CAST(SUBSTR(results.time,4,2) AS INTEGER)*60 + CAST(SUBSTR(results.time,7,2) AS INTEGER) + CAST(SUBSTR(results.time,10,3) AS INTEGER)/1000.0)) AS avg_champion_time_seconds FROM results JOIN races ON results.raceId = races.raceId JOIN driverStandings ON results.raceId = driverStandings.raceId AND results.driverId = driverStandings.driverId WHERE driverStandings.position = 1 AND results.time IS NOT NULL AND races.year < 1975 GROUP BY races.year ORDER BY races.year;

SELECT races.year, AVG((CAST(substr(results.time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600 + CAST(substr(results.time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60 + CAST(substr(results.time, 7, 2) AS INTEGER) + CAST(substr(results.time, 10, 3) AS INTEGER) / 1000.0)) AS avg_time_seconds FROM results JOIN races ON results.raceId = races.raceId JOIN driverStandings ON results.driverId = driverStandings.driverId AND results.raceId = driverStandings.raceId WHERE driverStandings.position = 1 AND results.time IS NOT NULL AND races.year < 1975 GROUP BY races.year ORDER BY races.year;

SELECT r.year, AVG((CAST(SUBSTR(res.time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600) + (CAST(SUBSTR(res.time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60) + CAST(SUBSTR(res.time, 7, 6) AS REAL)) AS avg_time_seconds FROM results res JOIN races r ON res.raceId = r.raceId JOIN driverStandings ds ON res.driverId = ds.driverId AND res.raceId = ds.raceId WHERE ds.position = 1 AND res.time IS NOT NULL AND r.year < 1975 GROUP BY r.year ORDER BY r.year;

SELECT s.year, AVG((CAST(substr(r.time, 1, 2) AS INTEGER) * 3600) + (CAST(substr(r.time, 4, 2) AS INTEGER) * 60) + CAST(substr(r.time, 7, 6) AS REAL)) AS avg_time_seconds FROM driverStandings ds JOIN results r ON ds.driverId = r.driverId AND ds.raceId = r.raceId JOIN races ra ON ra.raceId = ds.raceId JOIN seasons s ON s.year = ra.year WHERE ds.position = 1 AND r.time IS NOT NULL AND s.year < 1975 GROUP BY s.year ORDER BY s.year;

SELECT races.year, AVG((CAST(SUBSTR(results.time,1,2) AS INTEGER) * 3600) + (CAST(SUBSTR(results.time,4,2) AS INTEGER) * 60) + CAST(SUBSTR(results.time,7,6) AS REAL)) AS avg_time_seconds FROM results JOIN races ON results.raceId = races.raceId JOIN driverStandings ON results.driverId = driverStandings.driverId AND results.raceId = driverStandings.raceId WHERE driverStandings.position = 1 AND results.time IS NOT NULL AND races.year < 1975 GROUP BY races.year ORDER BY races.year;

thrombosis_prediction||1149.question: Are there more in-patient or outpatient who were male? What is the deviation in percentage?
thrombosis_prediction||1149.evidence: male refers to SEX = 'M'; in-patient refers to Admission = '+'; outpatient refers to Admission = '-'; percentage = DIVIDE(COUNT(ID) where SEX = 'M' and Admission = '+', COUNT(ID) where SEX  = 'M' and Admission = '-')
thrombosis_prediction||1149.gold: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN Admission = '+' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / SUM(CASE WHEN Admission = '-' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M'
samples: SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0)) * 100 AS Percentage_Deviation FROM Patient;

SELECT (CAST((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+') AS FLOAT) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-')) * 100 AS percentage_ratio, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+') - (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-') AS difference;

SELECT (SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+') AS InPatientCount, (SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-') AS OutPatientCount, (CAST((SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+') AS FLOAT) / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-'), 0)) * 100 AS PercentageDeviation;

SELECT (CAST((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+') AS FLOAT) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-')) * 100 AS percentage_inpatient_to_outpatient;

SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0)) * 100 AS deviation_percentage FROM Patient;

SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),0) - 1) * 100 AS deviation_percentage FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M';

SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0)) AS Male_Inpatient_to_Outpatient_Percentage FROM Patient;

SELECT (SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+') AS InPatientCount, (SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-') AS OutPatientCount, ((CAST((SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '+') AS FLOAT) / NULLIF((SELECT COUNT(ID) FROM Patient WHERE SEX = 'M' AND Admission = '-'), 0)) * 100) AS PercentageDeviation;

student_club||1359.gold: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff' THEN T1.amount ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / SUM(CASE WHEN T2.event_name = 'October Meeting' THEN T1.amount ELSE 0 END) FROM budget AS T1 INNER JOIN event AS T2 ON T1.link_to_event = T2.event_id WHERE T1.category = 'Advertisement' AND T2.type = 'Meeting'
samples: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT b1.link_to_event FROM budget b1 JOIN event e1 ON b1.link_to_event = e1.event_id JOIN budget b2 ON b1.category = 'Advertisement' AND b2.category = 'Advertisement' AND b1.link_to_event != b2.link_to_event JOIN event e2 ON b2.link_to_event = e2.event_id WHERE e1.event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff' AND e2.event_name = 'October Meeting' GROUP BY b1.link_to_event HAVING SUM(b1.amount) > SUM(b2.amount)) AS subquery;

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT b1.link_to_event FROM budget b1 JOIN event e1 ON b1.link_to_event = e1.event_id JOIN budget b2 ON b2.category = 'Advertisement' JOIN event e2 ON b2.link_to_event = e2.event_id WHERE b1.category = 'Advertisement' AND e1.event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff' AND e2.event_name = 'October Meeting' GROUP BY b1.link_to_event HAVING SUM(b1.amount) > SUM(b2.amount)) AS subquery;

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT SUM(b1.amount) AS yearly_kickoff_amount, SUM(b2.amount) AS october_meeting_amount FROM budget b1 JOIN event e1 ON b1.link_to_event = e1.event_id AND e1.event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff' AND b1.category = 'Advertisement' JOIN budget b2 ON b2.link_to_event = (SELECT event_id FROM event WHERE event_name = 'October Meeting' LIMIT 1) AND b2.category = 'Advertisement' HAVING yearly_kickoff_amount > october_meeting_amount) AS comparison;

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT event_name, SUM(amount) AS total_amount FROM budget JOIN event ON budget.link_to_event = event.event_id WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND event_name IN ('Yearly Kickoff', 'October Meeting') GROUP BY event_name) AS subquery WHERE (SELECT total_amount FROM subquery WHERE event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff') > (SELECT total_amount FROM subquery WHERE event_name = 'October Meeting');

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT b1.link_to_event FROM budget b1 JOIN event e1 ON b1.link_to_event = e1.event_id JOIN budget b2 ON b2.category = 'Advertisement' JOIN event e2 ON b2.link_to_event = e2.event_id WHERE b1.category = 'Advertisement' AND e1.event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff' AND e2.event_name = 'October Meeting' GROUP BY b1.link_to_event HAVING SUM(b1.amount) > SUM(b2.amount)) AS subquery;

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT CASE WHEN yk.amount > om.amount THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS comparison FROM (SELECT SUM(amount) AS amount FROM budget JOIN event ON budget.link_to_event = event.event_id WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND event.event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff') yk, (SELECT SUM(amount) AS amount FROM budget JOIN event ON budget.link_to_event = event.event_id WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND event.event_name = 'October Meeting') om) WHERE comparison = 1;

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM budget JOIN event ON budget.link_to_event = event.event_id WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND event.event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff') > (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM budget JOIN event ON budget.link_to_event = event.event_id WHERE category = 'Advertisement' AND event.event_name = 'October Meeting') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_more) WHERE is_more = 1;

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT b1.link_to_event FROM budget b1 JOIN event e1 ON b1.link_to_event = e1.event_id JOIN budget b2 ON b2.category = 'Advertisement' JOIN event e2 ON b2.link_to_event = e2.event_id WHERE b1.category = 'Advertisement' AND e1.event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff' AND e2.event_name = 'October Meeting' GROUP BY b1.link_to_event HAVING SUM(b1.amount) > SUM(b2.amount)) AS subquery;

student_club||1360.question: What percentage was the budget for Parking to the total budget for the "November Speaker"?

student_club||1360.gold: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.category = 'Parking' THEN T1.amount ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / SUM(T1.amount) FROM budget AS T1 INNER JOIN event AS T2 ON T1.link_to_event = T2.event_id WHERE T2.event_name = 'November Speaker'
samples: SELECT (SUM(b.amount) * 100.0) / (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM budget b2 JOIN event e2 ON b2.link_to_event = e2.event_id WHERE e2.event_name = 'November Speaker') AS parking_percentage FROM budget b JOIN event e ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id WHERE b.category = 'Parking' AND e.event_name = 'November Speaker'

SELECT (SUM(b.amount) * 100.0) / (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM budget b2 JOIN event e2 ON b2.link_to_event = e2.event_id WHERE e2.event_name = 'November Speaker') AS parking_percentage FROM budget b JOIN event e ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id WHERE b.category = 'Parking' AND e.event_name = 'November Speaker'

SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN category = 'Parking' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) * 100.0) / SUM(amount) FROM budget JOIN event ON budget.link_to_event = event.event_id WHERE event_name = 'November Speaker'

SELECT (SUM(b.amount) * 100.0) / (SELECT SUM(b2.amount) FROM budget b2 JOIN event e2 ON b2.link_to_event = e2.event_id WHERE e2.event_name = 'November Speaker') AS parking_percentage FROM budget b JOIN event e ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id WHERE b.category = 'Parking' AND e.event_name = 'November Speaker'

SELECT (SUM(budget.amount) FILTER (WHERE budget.category = 'Parking' AND event.event_name = 'November Speaker') * 100.0) / SUM(budget.amount) FILTER (WHERE event.event_name = 'November Speaker') AS parking_percentage FROM budget JOIN event ON budget.link_to_event = event.event_id WHERE event.event_name = 'November Speaker'

SELECT (SUM(b.amount) * 100.0) / (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM budget b2 JOIN event e2 ON b2.link_to_event = e2.event_id WHERE e2.event_name = 'November Speaker') AS parking_percentage FROM budget b JOIN event e ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id WHERE b.category = 'Parking' AND e.event_name = 'November Speaker';

SELECT (SUM(b.amount) * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM budget b2 JOIN event e2 ON b2.link_to_event = e2.event_id WHERE e2.event_name = 'November Speaker')) AS parking_percentage FROM budget b JOIN event e ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id WHERE b.category = 'Parking' AND e.event_name = 'November Speaker'

debit_card_specializing||1482.question: Which of the three segments—SME, LAM and KAM—has the biggest and lowest percentage increases in consumption paid in EUR between 2012 and 2013?

debit_card_specializing||1522.question: There's one customer spent 214582.17 in the June of 2013, which currency did he/she use?
debit_card_specializing||1522.evidence: June of 2013 means Date contains '201306' in the yearmonth.date of the database
debit_card_specializing||1522.gold: SELECT T2.Currency FROM yearmonth AS T1 INNER JOIN customers AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE T1.Date = '201306' AND T1.Consumption = 214582.17
samples: SELECT c.Currency FROM customers c JOIN (SELECT CustomerID, SUM(Amount * Price) AS TotalSpent FROM transactions_1k WHERE Date LIKE '201306%' GROUP BY CustomerID) t ON c.CustomerID = t.CustomerID WHERE TotalSpent = 214582.17 LIMIT 1;

SELECT c.Currency FROM customers c JOIN (SELECT CustomerID, SUM(Amount * Price) AS TotalSpent FROM transactions_1k WHERE Date LIKE '2013-06%' GROUP BY CustomerID HAVING TotalSpent = 214582.17) t ON c.CustomerID = t.CustomerID;
SELECT c.Currency FROM customers c JOIN yearmonth y ON c.CustomerID = y.CustomerID WHERE y.Date LIKE '201306%' GROUP BY c.CustomerID, c.Currency HAVING SUM(y.Consumption) = 214582.17 LIMIT 1;
SELECT c.Currency FROM customers c JOIN yearmonth y ON c.CustomerID = y.CustomerID WHERE y.Date LIKE '201306%' GROUP BY c.Currency HAVING SUM(y.Consumption) = 214582.17 LIMIT 1;

SELECT c.Currency FROM customers c JOIN (SELECT CustomerID, SUM(Price * Amount) AS TotalSpent FROM transactions_1k WHERE Date LIKE '2013-06%' GROUP BY CustomerID) t ON c.CustomerID = t.CustomerID WHERE TotalSpent = 214582.17 LIMIT 1;

SELECT c.Currency FROM customers c JOIN (SELECT CustomerID, SUM(Consumption) AS TotalSpent FROM yearmonth WHERE Date LIKE '201306%' GROUP BY CustomerID) y ON c.CustomerID = y.CustomerID WHERE y.TotalSpent = 214582.17 LIMIT 1;
